cards.back: The Cartesian Product of two sets A and B is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a is an element of A and b is an element of B.

cards.front: What is the definition of a Relational Symbol?
cards.back: A Relational Symbol, denoted by R, is a symbol that represents a relation between elements of two or more sets.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Cartesian Product in Relational Algebra?
cards.back: The Cartesian Product is used to create a new relation that contains all possible combinations of elements from two or more relations.

cards.front: What is the difference between a Relation and a Function?
cards.back: A Relation is a subset of the Cartesian Product of two sets, while a Function is a special type of Relation where each element in the domain maps to exactly one element in the range.

cards.front: What is the notation for the Cartesian Product of two sets A and B?
cards.back: The notation for the Cartesian Product of two sets A and B is A × B or A[B].
